data.title: Cardano Founder Pushes Back Against “Clarity at Any Cost” Crypto Regulation
data.description:
A public dispute has emerged between Charles Hoskinson and Brad Garlinghouse, exposing a deeper divide within the crypto industry over how to approach U. S. regulation. The disagreement centers on the Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act and whether the sector should accept imperfect legislation to end prolonged uncertainty or reject it outright to protect decentralization
source: https://www.ethnews.com/cardano-founder-pushes-back-against-clarity-at-any-cost-crypto-regulation/

data.title: CLARITY Act Reshapes Stablecoin Reward Structures
data.description: The CLARITY Act's Section 404 introduces significant changes to stablecoin platforms by prohibiting yield payments solely for holding stablecoins, aiming to prevent them from functioning like traditional bank deposits. According to NS3.AI, the legislation allows rewards linked to active participation, including transactions, liquidity provision, or governance activities, thereby transforming the structure of crypto rewards. Additionally, the Act requires transparent disclosures and restricts issuer involvement in reward programs, which may present compliance challenges and impact platform partnerships.

data.title: Ledger Plans $4 Billion IPO on NYSE in 2026
data.description: Ledger, a prominent manufacturer of cryptocurrency hardware wallets, is reportedly preparing for a $4 billion initial public offering (IPO) on the New York Stock Exchange in 2026. According to NS3.AI, this strategic move reflects the company's confidence in the rising demand for secure crypto storage solutions. Ledger's decision to pursue an IPO aims to leverage the growing interest from both institutional and retail sectors in cryptocurrency custody. The company is positioning itself to capitalize on the expanding market for secure digital asset management.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Interest Rate Decision May Impact Bitcoin and Risk Assets
data.description: The Federal Reserve is poised to make a decision on interest rates that could result in a 'dovish pause,' indicating a temporary halt in rate hikes. According to NS3.AI, Federal Reserve Chair Powell's comments on economic matters may temper any immediate positive effects on Bitcoin and other risk assets. Investors are advised to brace for cautious optimism while remaining aware of potential uncertainties following the announcement.

data.title: Tezos Implements Tallinn Upgrade to Enhance Blockchain Efficiency
data.description:
Tezos, a layer-1 proof-of-stake blockchain network, has implemented its latest protocol upgrade, known as Tallinn, which reduces block times on the base layer to six seconds. According to Cointelegraph, this marks the 20th update to the protocol, aiming to enhance network performance by reducing block times, slashing storage costs, and decreasing latency, ultimately leading to faster network finality times.

The Tallinn upgrade introduces significant changes, allowing all network validators, referred to as 'bakers,' to attest to every block rather than a subset, as was the case in previous protocol versions. This improvement is achieved through BLS cryptographic signatures, which aggregate numerous signatures into a single one per block. By reducing the load on nodes, this development paves the way for further block time reductions. Additionally, the upgrade features an address indexing mechanism that eliminates redundant address data, thereby reducing storage requirements for applications operating on Tezos. Tezos representatives highlighted that this mechanism enhances storage efficiency by a factor of 100.

The latest upgrade underscores the ongoing efforts to develop faster and higher-throughput blockchain networks capable of handling more transactions per second and reducing settlement times to support a growing array of use cases. Historically, the first generation of blockchain networks, such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, had transaction speeds of approximately seven transactions per second (TPS) and 15-30 TPS, respectively. Bitcoin's protocol, which produces blocks approximately every 10 minutes, poses challenges for everyday payments and commercial transactions on the base layer. This has led to the development of layer-2 (L2) networks to scale transaction execution. For Bitcoin, this is achieved through the Lightning Network, which facilitates off-chain transactions between parties, posting only the net balance to the base layer once the payment channel is closed. Ethereum, on the other hand, employs a modular approach with an ecosystem of L2 networks, separating execution, consensus, and data availability layers. In contrast, monolithic blockchain networks like Solana integrate all these functions into a single layer, foregoing the need for L2 scaling solutions.

data.title: Trump’s second term posts weakest stock performance in two decades
data.description:
The stock market delivered positive returns during Donald Trump’s first year back as president, but the gains fell short compared to other recent presidential terms, marking the slowest start for any president in two decades. Market indexes climbed 13. 3% between inauguration day and January 20, 2026, according to data from CFRA Research seen by CNN. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trumps-second-term-posts-weakest-stock-performance-in-two-decades/

data.title: Tezos Tallinn upgrade now live, slashes block times to 6 seconds
data.description:
The Tallinn protocol upgrade marks Tezos' 20th major update since launching in 2018, and was implemented without a network fork.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/tezos-tallinn-upgrade-blocks-6-seconds?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
